summary: Tested YouTube OAuth popup-flow refactor (Configuración page) + Importador integration. All backend endpoints + UI states validated. Manual import flow still functions correctly.
verified_features: GET /api/youtube/auth-url?redirect_uri=... returns auth_url containing access_type=offline and prompt=select_account (credentials are configured in this env, so 200 was returned)
POST /api/youtube/callback validates input (422 when missing body, 400 on invalid code)
DELETE /api/youtube/disconnect returns {success:true} and removes token doc (auth-status subsequently returns authenticated:false)
Configuración page renders new 'CUENTA DE YOUTUBE' section
When not authenticated: 'Conectar cuenta de YouTube' button visible with data-testid='connect-youtube-account-button'

Clicking Conectar triggers window.open (verified via JS hook) to https://accounts.google.com/o/oauth2/auth with response_type=code, access_type=offline, prompt=select_account, redirect_uri=/youtube-callback.html, and youtube scopes

When authenticated (seeded fake token): shows 'Cuenta conectada', email (test@example.com), channel ('Canal: Test Channel'), 'Cambiar de cuenta' and 'Desconectar' (data-testid='disconnect-youtube-button') buttons

Importador /importar 'Desde YouTube' tab when not authenticated shows 'Ir a Configuración' button (data-testid='go-to-config-button') which navigates to /configuracion — old Conectar button is removed
Importador when authenticated shows 'YouTube conectado' + connected email (data-testid='importer-youtube-email'), date range inputs (Desde/Hasta), and 'Descargar Comentarios' button
Manual import tab: pasting 2 YouTube-formatted comments → clicking 'Importar Comentarios' → toast '2 preguntas importadas' shown and a batch (Lote ID) is created
not_fully_tested: End-to-end real Google OAuth flow (cannot be tested without real user credentials — out of scope)

Disabled-state of Conectar button when YouTube Client ID/Secret not configured — in this env the credentials ARE set, so the disabled+warning branch could not be observed. Code review of Configuracion.jsx shows the branch exists and is gated on youtubeClientId/Secret settings.
critical_code_review_comments: Configuración page has both a 'YouTube API (Opcional)' credentials section and a 'CUENTA DE YOUTUBE' connection section — flow is clear, but consider disabling 'Conectar' button + showing a helper link if Client ID/Secret inputs are empty (currently user can click and then get a toast error). Code does implement a disabled branch but only based on settings already persisted — not on the in-memory form field; a user who just typed credentials but hasn't clicked 'Guardar Configuración' could hit the 400.

Backend /api/youtube/callback catches all exceptions and returns a generic 400 — OK, but logs full exception. Consider validating `state` matches a stored value in db.youtube_oauth_states for CSRF safety (currently state is stored but not verified on callback).
test_credentials: N/A – MongoDB direct seed used: type:'user_token', account_email:'test@example.com', channel_title:'Test Channel' (cleaned up after test)
seed_data_creation: Inserted a fake youtube_tokens doc into MongoDB to simulate authenticated UI state. Cleaned up after testing (deleted the token + any test questions created during manual import).
context_for_next_testing_agent: YouTube OAuth popup flow is fully wired: window.open from Configuracion.jsx → /youtube-callback.html → POST /api/youtube/callback → token saved in MongoDB collection youtube_tokens with type=user_token. Importador uses the same token automatically. To simulate authenticated state, seed db.youtube_tokens with {type:'user_token', account_email, channel_title, token, refresh_token, expiry:<future ISO>}. Cleanup with db.youtube_tokens.delete_many({type:'user_token'}).
